question: Will Coinbase support Polygon USDC deposits & withdrawals by June 30, 2022?

evidences.title: Will Coinbase support Polygon USDC deposits & withdrawals by ...
evidences.content: # Will Coinbase support Polygon USDC deposits & withdrawals by June 30, 2022? [...] This is a market on whether Coinbase will support USDC deposits and withdrawals on the Polygon mainnet by June 30, 2022. Coinbase Pro, and Coinbase Wallet are not considered for this market - the main Coinbase site needs to support USDC deposits and withdrawals on the Polygon mainnet for this market to resolve to "Yes." This market will resolve to "Yes" if by the resolution time June 30, 2022 (11:59:59 PM ET) Coinbase users are able to deposit and withdraw USDC to and from their Coinbase account (aka custodial wallet) through the Polygon mainnet network. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". The resolution source is the Coinbase platform and Coinbase blog ( If the resolution criteria are satisfied prior to the resolution date, this market will resolve immediately. [...] # Will Coinbase support Polygon USDC deposits & withdrawals by June 30, 2022? evidences.content: By Nemil Dalal, Justin Blumenthal Jun 23, 2022 • 5min read TLDR: Over the next month, eligible Coinbase customers will be able to send and receive ETH, MATIC, and USDC on Polygon, and USDC on Solana. The Polygon integration marks the first time Coinbase has enabled the ability to send and receive these assets on an L2 or sidechain. Image 7: Send and receive crypto on multiple networks, starting with Polygon and Solana _[Updated 9_ _/23] For a full list of assets with multi-chain support, see__here__._ _[Updated 11/23] Coinbase customers can now send and receive__ETH__,__DAI__, and WBTC on Optimism. Some large deposits and deposits made during periods of extremely high bridging activity may be subject to delays, learn more__here__._ [...] Institutional traders are increasingly active across multiple networks and dapps, but the process of bridging between networks is inefficient for them as well. Multichain asset support via Coinbase Exchange allows for seamless deposits and withdrawals across Ethereum, Polygon, and Solana, with trading and settlement in one order book regardless of the chain used to deposit funds. evidences.title: Send and receive crypto on multiple networks, starting with Polygon and Solana
evidences.content: TLDR: Over the next month, eligible Coinbase customers will be able to send and receive ETH, MATIC, and USDC on Polygon, and USDC on Solana. While these high gas fees have caused more affordable networks like Polygon and Solana to increase in popularity over the past 18 months, the process of funding wallets on these networks can be complex and time-consuming. Coinbase is reducing the time, effort, and high fees of today’s experience by letting customers convert fiat to crypto and fund their Polygon and Solana wallets in minutes and at a fraction of the cost. Over the next month, eligible Coinbase and Coinbase Exchange customers will be able to send and receive ETH, MATIC, and USDC on Polygon, and they’ll be able to send and receive USDC on Solana.¹ Over time, we’ll add support for more tokens and more networks. On Ethereum, it can cost over $10 in gas fees to send small amounts of crypto from an exchange like Coinbase to a self-custodial wallet like Coinbase Wallet, and it can cost over $100 in gas to lend out larger amounts of crypto on protocols like Aave. As a result, it can take approximately 20 minutes, $50 in gas, and 10 protracted steps to move your assets from ETH to Po...

evidences.title: Send USDC to Coinbase on Polygon: A Step-by-Step Guide - Bitget
evidences.content: ### - Use Bitget Wallet for Maximum Compatibility Bitget Wallet simplifies sending and receiving USDC on Polygon, and is well recognized for its security, multi-chain support, and user interface. ### - Small Test Amount First If it’s your first time transferring USDC to Coinbase on Polygon, send a small test amount first. Ensure it arrives safely before moving larger balances. ### - Understand Fees Polygon transactions are relatively low-fee, but double-check the estimated gas fee before confirming. Coinbase generally does not charge a deposit fee for USDC. ### - Double-Check Tag/Memo Requirements For USDC on Polygon, Coinbase typically does not require a wallet tag or memo. However, always confirm as platform rules change. ### - Watch Out for Network Mismatches [...] Before you start, make sure you have: ### 2. Double-Check Network Compatibility ⚠️ Important: Sending USDC on an unsupported network can result in permanent loss. Always ensure that both sender and receiver support Polygon for USDC transfers. ### 3. Get Your Coinbase Deposit Address and Wallet Tag (if required) ### 4. Initiate the Transfer From Bitget Wallet ### 5. Confirm Transaction on the Blockchain ### 6. Check...

evidences.title: Assets on multiple networks - Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Ethereum on Polygon is called Wrapped Ethereum (WETH). ### I have wrapped MATIC (wMATIC). Can I send that to Coinbase? No, Coinbase doesn’t support wMATIC. If you send wMATIC to Coinbase, your funds will be lost. ### I deposited USDC-SPL to Coinbase using the Solana network. Where is it in my Coinbase account? When USDC-SPL is deposited from the Solana network, it is reflected in your USDC balance. Related articles: Automatic return for unsupported network transactions # Need more help? 󰈉 [...] Yes, but only for assets that have multi-chain support. If Coinbase supports sending or receiving an asset on both the Ethereum and the Polygon network, then you will have one address that you can use for either network. For all other assets that are supported only on the Polygon or the Ethereum network, select the correct network to avoid lost funds. ### Can I use Ethereum for gas on the Polygon network? No. MATIC is used for gas on the Polygon network. You can send crypto to your wallet on the Polygon network without MATIC, but you must have MATIC in your wallet to make subsequent transactions or use dapps on the Polygon network. You can buy MATIC on Coinbase. ### I sent ETH to my walle...

evidences.title: USDC surpasses $600 million in market capitalization and MakerDAO adopts USDC as collateral
evidences.content: Title: USDC surpasses $600 million in market capitalization and MakerDAO adopts USDC as collateral # USDC surpasses $600 million in market capitalization and MakerDAO adopts USDC as collateral. **USDC** **is a fully collateralized US dollar stablecoin, created by the** **CENTRE Consortium****, an open source technology project co-founded by Coinbase and** **Circle****. Since its launch in 2018,** **USDC** **has seen its market share steadily climb to become the second largest stablecoin in the world by market capitalization, in direct correlation with increased consumer demand for a stable, secure, and transparent digital dollar.**. **USDC** **surpasses $600 million in market capitalization**. USDC** **allows the unbanked and underbanked anywhere in the world to hold a US dollar backed digital asset with a mobile phone. In recent volatile market conditions, we’re also seeing more and more consumers and institutions turn to USDC** **as a trusted means to weather the turbulence. It’s free to buy USDC** **with a bank account, and Coinbase customers can convert their USDC** **into any supported cryptocurrencies on Coinbase.com or convert and withdraw in US dollars anytime. Further,...

evidences.title: Coinbase USDC Bootstrap Fund invests in Uniswap and PoolTogether
evidences.content: # Coinbase USDC Bootstrap Fund invests in Uniswap and PoolTogether. In 2020, these protocols are starting to provide global access to financial services like savings and exchange. To encourage growth in USDC and DeFi, Coinbase launched the USDC Bootstrap Fund last September with $2MM of initial funding. The USDC Bootstrap Fund invests directly into smart contract protocols, providing important, early liquidity for entrepreneurs and developers looking to grow their protocol. With USDC, we hope to provide critical infrastructure that will enable DeFi to grow and increasingly compete with existing financial products. In addition to our publicly announced investments in Compound and dYdX, we are proud to announce further investments in Uniswap and PoolTogether. Uniswap is emerging as one of the most liquid exchanges on Ethereum. Their constant market maker model makes it easy to launch a liquid exchange for any token, and is increasingly becoming critical infrastructure for tokens and applications that need liquidity. In a little over a year, Uniswap has grown from $0 to $31MM of liquidity, and now has exchanges (also known as “pools”) for well over 880 tokens. The USDC Bootstrap Fu...

evidences.title: USDC payment processing in Coinbase Commerce
evidences.content: Coinbase Developer Platform 󰅖󰙂 Payments Fast and global stablecoin payments with a single integration󰀤 Trading Launch crypto trading and custody for your users󰣨 Wallets Deploy customizable and scalable wallets for your business󰋨 Stablecoins Access USDC and Coinbase Custom Stablecoins. When a customer wants to pay to a Coinbase Commerce merchant, we create a charge object that keeps track of what the customer wants to buy, how much it costs, the blockchain address they need to pay to, and other metadata. Our solution is based on the following idea: We create smart contracts that can accept funds as if they were ordinary accounts, with the sole functionality of forwarding the accepted funds to a fixed, predetermined address that belongs to the merchant. For each merchant who has enabled USDC payments, we would need to preemptively deploy multiple forwarders to the blockchain to ensure that there is an available pool of addresses when a customer initiates a payment flow. After we detect a payment to one of the deployed contracts, we (or the merchant) can call the flush function to forward the tokens to the merchant’s destination and finalize the movement of funds. If a customer pay...

evidences.content: Although not directly used in these ETFs, Circle’s longstanding regulatory-first posture means USDC is benefitting from the broader digital asset market trends of greater regulatory clarity and integration into the global financial system. Major traditional investment platforms in the U.S. and around the world continue to expand their digital offerings, with USDC as the bridge that connects traditional and digital asset markets for their customers. Coinbase logo #### Coinbase [...] Coinbase logo #### Coinbase ‍Coinbase provides a trusted platform that makes it easy for people and institutions to engage with digital assets, including trading, staking, safekeeping, spending, and fast, free global transfers. USDC plays a major role at Coinbase, comprising a significant share of trading liquidity and collateral. In 2023, Coinbase launched Base, an Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain that enables USDC transactions in less than a second, at a cost of less than a penny. This year, Base saw notable adoption growth and USDC — as the leading stablecoin on Base — was a key part of that surge. evidences.title: Polygon (MATIC), SKALE (SKL) and SushiSwap (SUSHI) are launching on Coinbase Pro
evidences.content: Title: Polygon (MATIC), SKALE (SKL) and SushiSwap (SUSHI) are launching on Coinbase Pro # Polygon (MATIC), SKALE (SKL) and SushiSwap (SUSHI) are launching on Coinbase Pro. Starting Today Tuesday March 9, transfer MATIC, SKL and SUSHI into your Coinbase Pro account ahead of trading. Support for MATIC, SKL and SUSHI will be available in all Coinbase’s supported jurisdictions, with the exception of SUSHI which will not be supported in New York State. Polygon (MATIC), SKALE (SKL) and SushiSwap (SUSHI) are launching on Coinbase Pro. One of the most common requests we receive from customers is to be able to trade more assets on our platform. Starting immediately, we will begin accepting inbound transfers of ***MATIC, SKL and SUSHI***to Coinbase Pro. Trading will begin on or after 9AM Pacific Time (PT) ***Thursday March 11***, if liquidity conditions are met. Once sufficient supply of ***MATIC, SKL and SUSHI*** is established on the platform, trading on our **MATIC-USD, MATIC-****BTC****, MATIC-EUR, MATIC-GBP, SKL-USD, SKL-****BTC****, SKL-EUR, SKL-GBP,SUSHI-USD, SUSHI-****BTC****, SUSHI-****ETH** **, SUSHI-EUR, and SUSHI-GBP**, order books will launch in three phases, post-only, limit...

evidences.title: Assets on multiple networks | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: # Assets on multiple networks. For certain assets, you can choose from more than one network for your Coinbase transactions. When an asset is supported on multiple networks, the available networks show during the send or receive steps. * Support for multiple networks isn’t available in New York. **Note**: The same network must be used on both the sending end and the receiving end unless the transaction involves the Polygon and Ethereum networks. ### Send on another network. A default network is already selected and is shown during your transaction. 2. Confirm that you understand the risk of receiving over a new network. 3. To avoid losing funds, always confirm that you and the recipient are using the same network. * Coinbase cannot recover funds that are lost from being sent or received using an incorrect network. ### Receive on another network. A default network is already selected and is shown above the QR code. 2. Once you confirm that you understand the risk of receiving over a new network, a new QR code and deposit address is generated. 3. To avoid losing funds, always confirm that you and the sender are using the same network. * Coinbase cannot recover funds that are lost...

evidences.title: Trade and manage DEX assets | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: | Network issues | You may experience transaction delays or failures during periods of high network traffic or if you have insufficient funds | | DEX trades with your bank account | • Because DEX trades involve sending USDC off Coinbase to complete your trade, we may place a temporary hold on the assets in your primary balance until your bank transfer is complete if you don't have a sufficient instant trading limit (typically up to 3-5 business days) • You can view your balances temporarily on hold from My Assets under Balance summary • You can still trade your assets on Coinbase instantly and will receive an email once the temporary hold is released | [...] ## Related articles DEX trading - Introduction DEX trading - Risks and safety [...] # Trade and manage DEX assets You can buy, sell, send, and receive DEX assets on the Coinbase app. You’ll create a required self-custody wallet prior to your first DEX trade to hold your DEX assets. DEX assets trade on a third-party decentralized exchange and haven't been reviewed by Coinbase.

evidences.title: Coinbase Card for the EU | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Coinbase Card for the EU | Coinbase Help. Coinbase Card for the EU. #### Apply for a Coinbase Card. Coinbase Card allows residents to spend crypto (including USDC) and local currency wherever Visa cards are accepted. **To to be eligible for the Coinbase Card:**. * Agree to the Coinbase Card legal terms. 3. Follow the prompts to apply for your Coinbase Card. In the meantime, you can start using your virtual card in the Coinbase mobile app. The Coinbase Card is issued by Pathward N.A., Member FDIC, pursuant to a license from Visa U.S.A. Inc. The Coinbase Card is powered by Marqeta.1Coinbase will automatically convert all cryptocurrency to US Dollars and transfer the funds to your Coinbase Card (less conversion fees) for use in purchases and ATM withdrawals.2Crypto rewards are an optional Coinbase offer.3Standard data charges may apply. #### Use your Coinbase Card. You can use your card immediately by viewing the number through your Coinbase account. Make sure the name on your Coinbase Card matches the name on the linked account. **To fund your Coinbase Card:**. #### Manage your Coinbase Card. You can manage many aspects of your Coinbase Card such requesting a new card, updating yo...

evidences.title: Sell crypto for cash | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: # Sell crypto for cash You can sell your crypto to your cash balance at any time without restriction.Based on the payment methods available to you, you can then transfer, withdraw, or leave your cash balance for future crypto purchases. Before you sell, make sure you have the latest version of the Coinbase app. Check your available balance and confirm selling to a cash balance is available to you. ### Browser 1. Select Sell. 2. Select the crypto you want to sell and the amount. 3. Update the To destination if needed. 4. This defaults to your local currency balance but you may have the option of adding to another balance (e.g., USDC). 5. Select Review order. 6. Select Sell now to complete the sale. Once the transaction is complete, the funds will be available in your cash balance. [...] ### Mobile app 1. Select Buy & Sell. 2. Select Sell. 3. Select the crypto you want to sell and the amount. 4. Update the To destination if needed. 5. This defaults to your local currency balance (USD) but you may have the option of adding to another balance (e.g., USDC). 6. Select Review order. 7. Select Sell now to complete the sale. Once the transaction is complete, the funds will be available i...

evidences.title: Coinbase One | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Title: Coinbase One | Coinbase Help # Coinbase One. Coinbase One is a monthly membership product offering members access to exclusive benefits, including zero trading fees, enhanced account protection, and boosted USDC and staking rewards. Try Coinbase One today and unlock zero trading fees! **Refer to the current list of eligible countries to see if Coinbase One is available in your area:**. ## Coinbase One tiers and benefits. Coinbase One offers three tiers, each with their own benefits:. | **Zero trading fees on simple trades** (crypto only; Coinbase Advanced, DEX fees, and derivatives excluded; a spread applies) | Up to $500 per month | Up to $10K per month | Unlimited |. | **25% rebate on Coinbase Advanced spot fees** | Not included | Up to $100 rebate per month | Unlimited |. | **Free wire transfers** Coinbase fees waived for domestic US wire transfers | Not included | Included | Included |. You can’t share Coinbase One with another customer. For security purposes, we encourage customers to maintain sole access to their individual Coinbase account. ## Sign up for Coinbase One. 1. evidences.title: Vaults | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Note: If you send crypto to the wrong crypto address (for example, Bitcoin to Bitcoin Cash address), your crypto will be lost. ### How do I withdraw funds from a vault? To initiate a withdrawal from your vault to your balance: 1. Go to My Assets 2. Select the asset type in which you'd like to access your account 3. Under the asset graph, select the Balance tab 4. Select See balance summary 5. Select Vault to view your vault 6. Enter the crypto amount you’d like to withdraw (you can select Withdraw all to transfer all your funds) 7. Follow the prompts to complete your withdrawal [...] For a standard vault, once you enter the withdrawal details, a confirmation email will be sent to your inbox and to your secondary email inbox. Once the details are approved there, the 48-hour waiting period will begin. Note: Currently, it's only possible to create one withdrawal request at a time. You must wait for an existing withdrawal to complete, or cancel it, before making another. ### Can I change the settings on a vault after it has been created? Once a vault has been created, its withdrawal delay period (48 hours), notification settings, security settings, and owner cannot be changed. In th...

evidences.title: Funding your account - EMEA | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Preview the transaction and confirm by clicking Withdraw cash now. SEPA deposits and withdrawals can take 1-3 business days. #### Deposit with Faster Payments (GBP) When depositing funds through Faster Payments, Coinbase automatically confirms if the name on your Coinbase account matches with your sending bank. You can only fund your account by linking bank accounts held solely in your name. Understanding name match results Match: The details you've entered match the bank account on file. Partial Match: You've entered a name similar to an account verified with the bank. What to do: Review your spelling and update the details as necessary. No Match: The provided details do not match any account verified with the bank. [...] What to do: Review your spelling and update the details as necessary. If you want to opt out of this feature, please contact our Support team). #### Deposit with USD SWIFT SWIFT (USD) deposit instructions, including bank account number, are unique to your Coinbase account. A transaction reference isn't necessary. You can make up to 3 deposits per day, with no limit on the USD amount. Banks currently NOT supported: CHINA MERCHANTS BANK, HONG KONG BRA BANK OF CH...
